title: AgentDecision
description:
Structured log entry for a single decision made by an LLM-driven agent. Every agent action that changes system state or enters the approval queue is logged here. Provides the audit trail for reproducibility, hallucination review, and the human-approval workflow.

Two agents produce AgentDecision records: the Search Strategist (decision_types: propose_experiments, triage_failure, write_pareto_rationale) and the Research Analyst (decision_type: ingest_paper). A decision_type must match its agent_name.

decision_type.description: What kind of decision this is. Must be consistent with agent_name: search_strategist owns propose_experiments/triage_failure/write_pareto_rationale; research_analyst owns ingest_paper.

input_hash.description: SHA-256 of the canonical JSON of the agent's input package (Pareto frontier snapshot, search state, paper reference, etc.). Enables reproducibility auditing — same input + same llm_id should produce the same or comparable output.

output_hash.description: SHA-256 of the canonical JSON of the agent's output (proposed ExperimentConfig list, triage result, rationale text, HypothesisRecord). Links this decision record to its produced artifact.

rationale.description: Free-form explanation from the LLM for this decision. The primary human-readable record of the agent's reasoning. Written for a developer reading the decision log, not for machine parsing.

confidence_flags.description: Per-field uncertainty flags from the agent. Keys are field or concept names the agent is uncertain about; values are the confidence level. Absence of a key means the agent did not flag uncertainty for that field. An empty object means no uncertainty was flagged.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone when the agent produced this decision.

llm_id.description: Identifier for the LLM that produced this decision (e.g. 'Qwen3-Coder-7B-Q4', 'claude-sonnet-4-5'). Distinct from ExperimentConfig.model_id, which identifies the VLM under evaluation. Records which model was reasoning, not which model was being tested.

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock time in milliseconds from input submission to output receipt. Useful for LLM latency tracking and budget accounting.
